findings:
Today's tape lands **three direct 5/5 matches**, all converging on the same 24-hour news cycle. The Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss scoop converted the Apr 27 / Apr 28 "Mag 7 prints + per-token-margin disclosure" predictions from "narrative confirmed, outcome pending" to "narrative crystallized into market reality" — the AI-trade tape moved as forecast, with Oracle -4%, AMD -3%, Broadcom -4%, and SoftBank -10% lining up directly under the per-token-margin-gating thesis. The FOMC presser and the four Mag 7 prints all land within 8 hours of this report, so the second-derivative confirmation is on the way.

The AI-security thesis line — Apr 26 "first AI Infra CVE class with NVD tagging" and Apr 28 "Inference-server SSTI graduates to OWASP LLM Top-10 / CISA AI-Infra KEV sub-catalog" — gets reinforced rather than resolved at 5/5. SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) **remains unpatched 10+ days post-disclosure** with a public PoC and the same Jinja2-without-`ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment` shape now confirmed across vLLM (CVE-2025-61620) and LMDeploy (CVE-2026-33626). The Apr 28 CVE wave (Milesight default-SSL-key + Totolink A8000RU triple, all CVSS 9.8) extends the Apr 27-28 cluster to 7+ distinct 9.8s in 72h, and CISA's same-day KEV add (ConnectWise ScreenConnect path-traversal already chained with auth-bypass for 14+ months + Windows Shell APT28 zero-day exploited since Dec 2025) shows the regulator-side pressure tightening on schedule. No dedicated AI-Infra sub-catalog has shipped yet, but the Q3-Q4 2026 forecast is on track.

The Apr 26 "Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling graduates to compute-stack lock-in" prediction picks up the day's most direct pull-forward signal: the WSJ scoop **explicitly cites Anthropic gaining ground on OpenAI in coding and enterprise**, which converts the "multi-frontier-vendor enterprise strategy" leg of the prediction from forecast to reality in a single news cycle. Anthropic-AWS Trainium + Graviton silicon-level co-engineering, Claude Cowork in Bedrock, and AgentCore Managed Harness reinforce the lock-in side at the same time.

The Apr 23 "Agent Control Plane" prediction lands at 5/5 again as Multica v0.2.18 (Apr 27) succeeds v0.2.16 on the same orchestrator-skills surface in the same window the **Novita Sandbox** launches as the first commercial agent-runtime sandbox purpose-built for OpenClaw + Hermes + autonomous systems. The OSS (Multica) and commercial (Novita Sandbox + AgentCore Managed Harness) sides of the control-plane category are now both shipping product into the AI Dev 26 SF window. Apr 25's "MCP attack-surface bottleneck → MCP Gateway / Firewall product wave" prediction tracks at 4/5 — Novita Sandbox is the first commercial mitigation product purpose-built for the agent-runtime threat surface, which is exactly the product class the prediction called for, just one level up the stack from MCP itself.

Apr 24's "1M context as default open-weight era" lifts to 4/5: DeepSeek V4-Pro/Flash 1M context carry-forward is reinforced by **Corpus2Skill (arxiv 2604.14572, Apr 27)** — which beats RAPTOR / dense retrieval / agentic RAG on WixQA **without a vector DB at serve time** — and **PolyKV (arxiv 2604.24971, Apr 27)** layering shared asymmetric KV-pool compression on top. The "vector DB redefines as long-context cache layer" leg of the prediction now has its first end-to-end alternative architecture (skill-tree navigation + compressed-cache pool) demonstrating measurable wins in published research on the same day.

Two predictions show low-relevance status today: Apr 22's "OAuth scope category carve-out" sits at 2 (the underlying IPI risk vector is reinforced but no SSO scope-category carve-out has happened) and Apr 23's "third-party-vendor boundary + URL-naming entropy" sits at 1 (no contractor-incident or URL-randomization mandate today; ClawHavoc is an adjacent skill-supply-chain class, not the contractor / preview-URL vector).

The dormant-pool entry **20260420-3 "Headless Everything architecture standardizes"** is not yet due for periodic ping (next 2026-05-11) and produced zero Layer 1 keyword hits and no Layer 2 semantic match against today's headlines / subheadings. No revival today.
relation_to_my_preds:
**On "malicious local LLMs functioning like malware + zero-trust as the fundamental safeguard":** Today's report carries the day's strongest signal yet on the *agent-skills-as-attack-surface* counterpart of your malicious-local-LLM thesis. **ClawHavoc's 1,184 malicious skills in OpenClaw's ClawHub** with self-erasing-after-install memory (Antiy Labs + Koi Security analysis + Kurtz RSAC keynote) plus **Cisco's RSAC 2026 finding of 36% prompt-injection-detectable across the OpenClaw skill marketplace** are the canonical real-world example of "internally driven multifunctional behaviors that may trigger breaches or data leakage" that you described — except the substrate is currently agent skills rather than fully autonomous local LLMs, which is the natural transition path. The zero-trust-as-fundamental-safeguard side of your prediction continues to land cleanly: Okta for AI Agents GA is **1 day out (Apr 30)** with Agent Discovery & Governance + Agent Gateway as a central MCP control plane + virtual MCP server registry + credential vaulting + auto-rotation + universal logout — exactly the "intentionally designed system paths AI alone cannot access" pattern you specified. **Apr 28 Novita Sandbox** is the first commercial agent-runtime sandbox purpose-built for OpenClaw + Hermes + autonomous systems, sitting in the Solutions: Zero-Trust Access Control section of today's report. The behavior-monitoring half of your prediction — "solutions that claim to monitor and manage behavior will likely attract attention in only the short term" — continues to track: Google + Forcepoint's IPI in-the-wild reports and OpenAI's public concession that prompt-injection is structurally permanent map onto the "doesn't overturn the bypass assumption" caveat you flagged.

**On "cloud APIs for advanced tasks + local LLMs for daily operations, driven by SaaS price rises":** Today's tape is the strongest demand-side signal so far for the price-rise leg of your prediction. The Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-miss explicitly framed the AI-capex tape's first material crack since Apr 17 — Friar reportedly told colleagues that compute-funding tightens without re-acceleration. The **per-token-margin-gating thesis** under today's Mag 7 prints + FOMC is essentially a leading indicator for the "service delivery models and pricing structures" change you predicted: when hyperscalers begin gating incremental GPU capex on Bedrock/Azure/Vertex per-token margins, downstream SaaS prices follow within 1-2 quarters. On the supply side, Apr 27-29 lands a coordinated push that materially strengthens the local-LLM half of your division-of-roles thesis: **TurboQuant + KV-cache-compression utility wave** (3.5-bit-per-value, 6x compression, 8x faster attention, FP16-matching on LongBench / Needle-in-Haystack) drops effective per-token cost ~5-6x for memory-bandwidth-bound long-context workloads, and **PrismML Bonsai-8B 1-bit at 44 tok/s on iPhone 17 Pro Max / 131 tok/s on M4 Pro / 368 tok/s on RTX 4090** + **Unsloth v0.1.37-beta + v0.1.36-beta Gemma-4-E2B 8 GB VRAM training** + **DeepSeek V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per M tokens** + **Qwen3.6-27B Apache-2.0 dense** keep building the local-side capability stack. The bifurcation infrastructure is being built; the explicit consumer-facing AI-SaaS price hike has not yet landed today, but the leading indicator is in motion.

**On "RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement becomes widespread":** Today's report carries the strongest indirect signal yet via the **Externalization in LLM Agents** survey (arxiv 2604.08224, Apr 24) framing the move from weights → prompts → skills/harness/protocol as the dominant 2026 design pattern, plus **Corpus2Skill** (arxiv 2604.14572, Apr 27) operationalizing skill-tree navigation. Once the harness/protocol layer is the locus of capability, RL/LLM-based optimization of *the harness itself* (which encompasses things like the future-prediction validation pipeline this scheduled task runs) becomes the natural research target. AgentCore Managed Harness's mid-session model-swap, per-session microVM, and Anthropic's Rate Limits API + Claude Managed Agents public beta provide the programmable substrate. No published RL-trained-forecaster paper landed today, but the surrounding infrastructure for "RL on the forecasting harness" is converging fast.
